---
title: "w6"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-15"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.




```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(GGally)
#install.packages("FNN")
library(FNN)
library(pROC)
library(caret)
```
















##Q1 Does availability_365 have an effect on Airbnb listing price?


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
#simple linear regression

library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(GGally)
library(FNN)

df <- read.csv("listings.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

df_clean <- df %>%
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::last_col()) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na()


df_availa <- df_clean %>%
  select(price, availability_365) %>%
  filter(!is.na(price), !is.na(availability_365))


df <- df_availa %>%
  mutate(log_price = log(price + 1))

ggplot(df, aes(x = availability_365, y = log_price)) +
  geom_point(color = "black", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Scatter plot of log(price + 1) vs availability_365",
    x = "availability_365",
    y = "log(price + 1)"
  )




```

#train and test
```{r}
df_vis <- df_clean %>%
  select(price, availability_365, number_of_reviews, reviews_per_month, room_type)
summary(df_vis)


#boxplot
ggplot(df_vis, aes(y = availability_365)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Boxplot of availability_365",
    y = "availability_365"
  )
ggplot(df_vis, aes(y = number_of_reviews)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "darkgreen") +
  labs(
    title = "Boxplot of number_of_reviews",
    y = "number_of_reviews"
  )


ggpairs(df_vis)


```


先观察数据集的变量,然后做可视化,最后做ggpairs看变量的相关性,发现numbers_of_review和reviews_per_month高度相关,然后做线性回归模型.  其他的做muti线性回归和分类模型


#@train and test 
```{r}
set.seed(1)

train_index <- sample(1:nrow(df_q1), size = 0.8 * nrow(df_q1))

train_data <- df_q1[train_index, ]
test_data  <- df_q1[-train_index, ]
```





#simple linear model, # Question 1  which means that availability_365 alone explains only a small proportion of the variation in log_price.
```{r}
df_q1 <- df_clean %>%
  select(price, availability_365) %>%
  filter(!is.na(price), !is.na(availability_365)) %>%
  mutate(log_price = log(price + 1))

set.seed(42)

train_index_q1 <- sample(1:nrow(df_q1), size = 0.8 * nrow(df_q1))

train_data <- df_q1[train_index_q1, ]
test_data  <- df_q1[-train_index_q1, ]

slr_q1 <- lm(log_price ~ availability_365, data = train_data)

summary(slr_q1)

ggplot(train_data, aes(x = availability_365, y = log_price)) +
  geom_point(color = "black", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Train set: log(price + 1) vs availability_365",
    x = "availability_365",
    y = "log(price + 1)"
  )

pred_q1 <- predict(slr_q1, newdata = test_data)

head(pred_q1)

mse_q1 <- mean((test_data$log_price - pred_q1)^2)
rmse_q1 <- sqrt(mse_q1)

mse_q1
rmse_q1

ggplot(data.frame(actual = test_data$log_price, predicted = pred_q1),
       aes(x = actual, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Test set: Actual vs Predicted log(price + 1)",
    x = "Actual log(price + 1)",
    y = "Predicted log(price + 1)"
  )

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(slr_q1)
```




#number of reviews and reviews_per_month
```{r}
df_q2 <- df_clean %>%
  select(number_of_reviews, reviews_per_month) %>%
  filter(!is.na(number_of_reviews), !is.na(reviews_per_month))

set.seed(42)

data_for_split <- df_q2

train_index <- sample(1:nrow(data_for_split), size = 0.8 * nrow(data_for_split))

train_data <- data_for_split[train_index, ]
test_data  <- data_for_split[-train_index, ]

slr_q2 <- lm(number_of_reviews ~ reviews_per_month, data = train_data)

summary(slr_q2)

ggplot(train_data, aes(x = reviews_per_month, y = number_of_reviews)) +
  geom_point(color = "black", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Train set: number_of_reviews vs reviews_per_month",
    x = "reviews_per_month",
    y = "number_of_reviews"
  )
pred_q2 <- predict(slr_q2, newdata = test_data)

head(pred_q2)

mse_q2 <- mean((test_data$number_of_reviews - pred_q2)^2)
rmse_q2 <- sqrt(mse_q2)

mse_q2
rmse_q2

ggplot(data.frame(actual = test_data$number_of_reviews, predicted = pred_q2),
       aes(x = actual, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Test set: Actual vs Predicted number_of_reviews",
    x = "Actual number_of_reviews",
    y = "Predicted number_of_reviews"
  )
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(slr_q2)

```

#add KNN to Q2, compare KNN with sLR
```{r}
set.seed(42)

n <- nrow(df_q2)

train_index <- sample(1:n, size = 0.8 * n)
remaining_index <- setdiff(1:n, train_index)
val_index <- sample(remaining_index, size = 0.1 * n)
test_index <- setdiff(1:n, c(train_index, val_index))

train_data <- df_q2[train_index, ]
val_data   <- df_q2[val_index, ]
test_data  <- df_q2[test_index, ]

k_values <- c(1, 3, 5, 10, 20, 30, 50, 75, 100)
val_mse <- numeric(length(k_values))

for (i in seq_along(k_values)) {
  k <- k_values[i]
  
  knn_pred_val <- knn.reg(
    train = data.frame(reviews_per_month = train_data$reviews_per_month),
    test  = data.frame(reviews_per_month = val_data$reviews_per_month),
    y     = train_data$number_of_reviews,
    k     = k
  )$pred
  
  val_mse[i] <- mean((val_data$number_of_reviews - knn_pred_val)^2)
}

data.frame(k = k_values, validation_MSE = val_mse)

best_k <- k_values[which.min(val_mse)]
best_k
ggplot(data.frame(k = k_values, validation_MSE = val_mse),
       aes(x = k, y = validation_MSE)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Validation MSE for different k values",
    x = "k",
    y = "Validation MSE"
  )

train_val_data <- rbind(train_data, val_data)

knn_pred_test <- knn.reg(
  train = data.frame(reviews_per_month = train_val_data$reviews_per_month),
  test  = data.frame(reviews_per_month = test_data$reviews_per_month),
  y     = train_val_data$number_of_reviews,
  k     = best_k
)$pred

mse_knn_q2 <- mean((test_data$number_of_reviews - knn_pred_test)^2)
rmse_knn_q2 <- sqrt(mse_knn_q2)

mse_knn_q2
rmse_knn_q2

ggplot(data.frame(actual = test_data$number_of_reviews, predicted = knn_pred_test),
       aes(x = actual, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = paste("KNN Test set: Actual vs Predicted number_of_reviews (k =", best_k, ")"),
    x = "Actual number_of_reviews",
    y = "Predicted number_of_reviews"
  )

x_grid <- seq(min(train_data$reviews_per_month),
              max(train_data$reviews_per_month),
              length.out = 200)

# SLR predictions
slr_line <- predict(slr_q2, newdata = data.frame(reviews_per_month = x_grid))

# KNN predictions
knn_line <- knn.reg(
  train = data.frame(reviews_per_month = train_val_data$reviews_per_month),
  test  = data.frame(reviews_per_month = x_grid),
  y     = train_val_data$number_of_reviews,
  k     = best_k
)$pred

plot_data <- data.frame(
  reviews_per_month = x_grid,
  slr_pred = slr_line,
  knn_pred = knn_line
)

ggplot(train_data, aes(x = reviews_per_month, y = number_of_reviews)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, color = "black") +
  geom_line(data = plot_data, aes(x = reviews_per_month, y = slr_pred),
            color = "purple", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(data = plot_data, aes(x = reviews_per_month, y = knn_pred),
            color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Simple Linear Regression vs KNN Regression",
    x = "reviews_per_month",
    y = "number_of_reviews"
  )
```



#MLR  same Q1 and Q2 but more vars
```{r}
df_mlr_q1 <- df_clean %>%
  select(price, availability_365, minimum_nights, number_of_reviews,
         reviews_per_month, calculated_host_listings_count, room_type) %>%
  mutate(log_price = log(price + 1))

set.seed(42)

data_for_split <- df_mlr_q1

train_index <- sample(1:nrow(data_for_split), size = 0.8 * nrow(data_for_split))

train_data <- data_for_split[train_index, ]
test_data  <- data_for_split[-train_index, ]

mlr_q1 <- lm(log_price ~ availability_365 + minimum_nights + number_of_reviews +
               reviews_per_month + calculated_host_listings_count + room_type,
             data = train_data)

summary(mlr_q1)

#test set
pred_mlr_q1 <- predict(mlr_q1, newdata = test_data)

head(pred_mlr_q1)


#MSE
mse_mlr_q1 <- mean((test_data$log_price - pred_mlr_q1)^2)
rmse_mlr_q1 <- sqrt(mse_mlr_q1)

mse_mlr_q1
rmse_mlr_q1

#test vs. train
ggplot(data.frame(actual = test_data$log_price, predicted = pred_mlr_q1),
       aes(x = actual, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "MLR Test set: Actual vs Predicted log(price + 1)",
    x = "Actual log(price + 1)",
    y = "Predicted log(price + 1)"
  )


comparison_price <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Simple Linear Regression", "Multiple Linear Regression"),
  MSE = c(mse_q1, mse_mlr_q1),
  RMSE = c(rmse_q1, rmse_mlr_q1)
)

comparison_price
```




#Q2 add some new features on train dataset   Q2
```{r}

#Nothing has been changed!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
df_mlr_q2 <- df_clean %>%
  select(number_of_reviews, reviews_per_month, availability_365,
         minimum_nights, calculated_host_listings_count, room_type)

set.seed(42)

data_for_split <- df_mlr_q2

train_index <- sample(1:nrow(data_for_split), size = 0.8 * nrow(data_for_split))

train_data <- data_for_split[train_index, ]
test_data  <- data_for_split[-train_index, ]

mlr_q2 <- lm(number_of_reviews ~ reviews_per_month + availability_365 +
               minimum_nights + calculated_host_listings_count + room_type,
             data = train_data)

summary(mlr_q2)

pred_mlr_q2 <- predict(mlr_q2, newdata = test_data)

head(pred_mlr_q2)

mse_mlr_q2 <- mean((test_data$number_of_reviews - pred_mlr_q2)^2)
rmse_mlr_q2 <- sqrt(mse_mlr_q2)

mse_mlr_q2
rmse_mlr_q2

ggplot(data.frame(actual = test_data$number_of_reviews, predicted = pred_mlr_q2),
       aes(x = actual, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "MLR Test set: Actual vs Predicted number_of_reviews",
    x = "Actual number_of_reviews",
    y = "Predicted number_of_reviews"
  )

comparison_reviews <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Simple Linear Regression", "Multiple Linear Regression"),
  MSE = c(mse_q2, mse_mlr_q2),
  RMSE = c(rmse_q2, rmse_mlr_q2)
)

comparison_reviews

```




#Logistic regression (simple) log(price)   Q1
```{r}

df_logistic_q1 <- df_clean %>%
  select(price, availability_365, minimum_nights, number_of_reviews, 
         reviews_per_month, calculated_host_listings_count, room_type) %>%
  mutate(high_price = ifelse(price >= median(price), 1, 0)) 

set.seed(42)

train_index <- sample(1:nrow(df_logistic_q1), size = 0.8 * nrow(df_logistic_q1))
train_data <- df_logistic_q1[train_index, ]
test_data  <- df_logistic_q1[-train_index, ]

slr_q1 <- glm(high_price ~ availability_365, data = train_data, family = binomial)
summary(slr_q1)



pred_prob <- predict(logreg.fit, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
pred_class <- ifelse(pred_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)
table(Predicted = pred_class, Actual = test_data$high_price)

accuracy <- mean(pred_class == test_data$high_price)
paste("Accuracy:", round(accuracy,4))


pred_q1_prob <- predict(slr_q1, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
roc_q1 <- roc(test_data$high_price, pred_q1_prob)
auc(roc_q1)

plot(roc_q1, col = "blue", main = "ROC Curve for Logistic Regression")


#============================
  


```





#Q1 muti 
```{r}

slr_q2 <- glm(high_price ~ availability_365 + minimum_nights + number_of_reviews + reviews_per_month + room_type + calculated_host_listings_count, data = train_data, family = binomial)
summary(slr_q2)


pred_prob2 <- predict(slr_q2, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
pred_class2 <- ifelse(pred_prob2 >= 0.5, 1, 0)

confusionMatrix(as.factor(pred_class2), as.factor(test_data$high_price),positive = "1")
pred_q1_prob <- predict(slr_q2, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
roc_q2 <- roc(test_data$high_price, pred_q1_prob)
auc(roc_q2)
plot(roc_q2, col = "blue", main = "ROC Curve for Logistic Regression")
```




#Q2 reviews
```{r}
df_logistic_q1 <- df_clean %>%
  select(number_of_reviews, reviews_per_month, availability_365,
          minimum_nights, calculated_host_listings_count, room_type) %>%
  mutate(high_reviews = ifelse(number_of_reviews >= median(number_of_reviews), 1, 0)) 

set.seed(42)

train_index <- sample(1:nrow(df_logistic_q1), size = 0.8 * nrow(df_logistic_q1))
train_data <- df_logistic_q1[train_index, ]
test_data  <- df_logistic_q1[-train_index, ]

slr_q3 <- glm(high_reviews ~ reviews_per_month, data = train_data, family = binomial)
summary(slr_q1)



pred_prob <- predict(slr_q3, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
pred_class <- ifelse(pred_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)
table(Predicted = pred_class, Actual = test_data$high_reviews)

accuracy <- mean(pred_class == test_data$high_reviews)
paste("Accuracy:", round(accuracy,4))


pred_q1_prob <- predict(slr_q3, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
roc_q1 <- roc(test_data$high_reviews, pred_q1_prob)
auc(roc_q1)

plot(roc_q1, col = "blue", main = "ROC Curve for Logistic Regression")
```





=============================





























































































```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
#simple linear regression

library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)


df <- read.csv("listings.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

df_clean <- df %>%
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::last_col()) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na()


df_availa <- df_clean %>%
  select(price, calculated_host_listings_count) %>%
  filter(!is.na(price), !is.na(calculated_host_listings_count))


df <- df_availa %>%
  mutate(log_price = log(price + 1))

ggplot(data = df, aes(x = calculated_host_listings_count, y = log_price)) +
  geom_point(color = "black", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Scatter plot of log(price + 1) vs calculated_host_listings_count",
    x = "calculated_host_listings_count",
    y = "log(price + 1)"
  )


```





```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(caret)
library(pROC)

df <- read.csv("listings.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Clean data
df_model <- df %>%
  select(-license) %>%
  drop_na(price, room_type, neighbourhood_group, latitude, longitude,
          minimum_nights, number_of_reviews, reviews_per_month,
          calculated_host_listings_count, availability_365, number_of_reviews_ltm) %>%
  mutate(
    log_price = log(price + 1),
    high_price = ifelse(price >= median(price), 1, 0)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 1. EDA
# -----------------------------
ggplot(df_model, aes(x = price)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  xlim(0, 1000) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of price", x = "Price", y = "Count")

ggplot(df_model, aes(x = log_price)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "darkgreen", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of log(price + 1)", x = "log(price + 1)", y = "Count")

ggplot(df_model, aes(x = availability_365, y = price)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  ylim(0, 1000) +
  labs(title = "Price vs availability_365", x = "availability_365", y = "price")

ggplot(df_model, aes(x = availability_365, y = log_price)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "red") +
  labs(title = "log(price + 1) vs availability_365", x = "availability_365", y = "log(price + 1)")

ggplot(df_model, aes(x = room_type, y = log_price)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "log(price + 1) by room type", x = "Room type", y = "log(price + 1)")

ggplot(df_model, aes(x = neighbourhood_group, y = log_price)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "log(price + 1) by neighbourhood group", x = "Neighbourhood group", y = "log(price + 1)")

# -----------------------------
# 2. Simple Linear Regression
# -----------------------------
slr_fit1 <- lm(log_price ~ availability_365, data = df_model)
summary(slr_fit1)

slr_fit2 <- lm(log_price ~ number_of_reviews, data = df_model)
summary(slr_fit2)

slr_fit3 <- lm(log_price ~ reviews_per_month, data = df_model)
summary(slr_fit3)

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(slr_fit1)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Train-test split
# -----------------------------
set.seed(1)
train_ind <- sample(1:nrow(df_model), size = 0.8 * nrow(df_model))

df_train <- df_model[train_ind, ]
df_test  <- df_model[-train_ind, ]

# -----------------------------
# 4. Multiple Linear Regression
# -----------------------------
mlr_fit <- lm(
  log_price ~ room_type + neighbourhood_group + latitude + longitude +
    minimum_nights + number_of_reviews + reviews_per_month +
    calculated_host_listings_count + availability_365 + number_of_reviews_ltm,
  data = df_train
)

summary(mlr_fit)

pred_mlr <- predict(mlr_fit, newdata = df_test)
mse_mlr <- mean((df_test$log_price - pred_mlr)^2)
rmse_mlr <- sqrt(mse_mlr)

mse_mlr
rmse_mlr

# -----------------------------
# 5. Simple Logistic Regression
# -----------------------------
logit_fit1 <- glm(high_price ~ availability_365, data = df_train, family = binomial)
summary(logit_fit1)

logit_fit_room <- glm(high_price ~ room_type, data = df_train, family = binomial)
summary(logit_fit_room)

# -----------------------------
# 6. Multiple Logistic Regression
# -----------------------------
logit_fit2 <- glm(
  high_price ~ room_type + neighbourhood_group + latitude + longitude +
    minimum_nights + number_of_reviews + reviews_per_month +
    calculated_host_listings_count + availability_365 + number_of_reviews_ltm,
  data = df_train,
  family = binomial
)

summary(logit_fit2)

pred_prob <- predict(logit_fit2, newdata = df_test, type = "response")
pred_class <- ifelse(pred_prob >= 0.5, 1, 0)

table(Predicted = pred_class, Actual = df_test$high_price)

confusionMatrix(as.factor(pred_class), as.factor(df_test$high_price), positive = "1")

roc_obj <- roc(df_test$high_price, pred_prob)
auc(roc_obj)

plot(roc_obj, col = "blue", main = "ROC Curve for Logistic Regression")

```







```{r}
############################################################
# STAT462 - listings.csv
# Simple Linear Regression
# Multiple Linear Regression
# Logistic Regression
# Binary Tree Classification
# Split: 80% train / 10% validation / 10% test
############################################################

# =========================
# 1. Load packages
# =========================
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(pROC)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)

# =========================
# 2. Read data
# =========================
listings <- read.csv("listings.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Check structure
str(listings)
summary(listings)

# =========================
# 3. Basic data cleaning
# =========================
# Keep variables that are useful for modelling
df <- listings %>%
  select(
    price,
    neighbourhood_group,
    room_type,
    latitude,
    longitude,
    minimum_nights,
    number_of_reviews,
    reviews_per_month,
    calculated_host_listings_count,
    availability_365,
    number_of_reviews_ltm
  )

# Convert types
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    price = as.numeric(price),
    neighbourhood_group = as.factor(neighbourhood_group),
    room_type = as.factor(room_type)
  )

# Remove rows with missing price
df <- df %>% filter(!is.na(price))

# Simple imputation for numeric variables
df$reviews_per_month[is.na(df$reviews_per_month)] <- median(df$reviews_per_month, na.rm = TRUE)

# Log-transform price for regression
df <- df %>%
  mutate(log_price = log(price + 1))

# Create binary target for classification:
# 1 = high price, 0 = low price
price_cutoff <- median(df$price, na.rm = TRUE)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(price_high = ifelse(price >= price_cutoff, 1, 0),
         price_high = as.factor(price_high))

# Check
summary(df)

# =========================
# 4. Train / Validation / Test split
# =========================
set.seed(462)

# First split: 80% train, 20% temp
train_index <- sample(1:nrow(df), size = 0.8 * nrow(df))

df_train <- df[train_index, ]
df_temp  <- df[-train_index, ]

# Second split temp into 10% validation + 10% test
set.seed(462)
valid_index <- sample(1:nrow(df_temp), size = 0.5 * nrow(df_temp))

df_valid <- df_temp[valid_index, ]
df_test  <- df_temp[-valid_index, ]

cat("Train size:", nrow(df_train), "\n")
cat("Validation size:", nrow(df_valid), "\n")
cat("Test size:", nrow(df_test), "\n")

# ============================================================
# PART A: SIMPLE LINEAR REGRESSION
# Predict log_price using one predictor
# ============================================================

# A simple model with one predictor
slr.fit <- lm(log_price ~ reviews_per_month, data = df_train)
summary(slr.fit)

# Predict on validation
slr.valid.pred <- predict(slr.fit, newdata = df_valid)
slr.valid.r2 <- cor(df_valid$log_price, slr.valid.pred)^2
slr.valid.rmse <- sqrt(mean((df_valid$log_price - slr.valid.pred)^2))

cat("\n--- Simple Linear Regression (Validation) ---\n")
cat("Validation R-squared:", round(slr.valid.r2, 4), "\n")
cat("Validation RMSE:", round(slr.valid.rmse, 4), "\n")

# Predict on test
slr.test.pred <- predict(slr.fit, newdata = df_test)
slr.test.r2 <- cor(df_test$log_price, slr.test.pred)^2
slr.test.rmse <- sqrt(mean((df_test$log_price - slr.test.pred)^2))

cat("\n--- Simple Linear Regression (Test) ---\n")
cat("Test R-squared:", round(slr.test.r2, 4), "\n")
cat("Test RMSE:", round(slr.test.rmse, 4), "\n")

# Plot
plot(df_train$reviews_per_month, df_train$log_price,
     main = "Simple Linear Regression: log_price ~ reviews_per_month",
     xlab = "reviews_per_month", ylab = "log_price")
abline(slr.fit, col = "red", lwd = 2)


# ============================================================
# PART B: MULTIPLE LINEAR REGRESSION
# Predict log_price using multiple predictors
# ============================================================

mlr.fit <- lm(
  log_price ~ neighbourhood_group + room_type + latitude + longitude +
    minimum_nights + number_of_reviews + reviews_per_month +
    calculated_host_listings_count + availability_365 + number_of_reviews_ltm,
  data = df_train
)

summary(mlr.fit)

# Validation
mlr.valid.pred <- predict(mlr.fit, newdata = df_valid)
mlr.valid.r2 <- cor(df_valid$log_price, mlr.valid.pred)^2
mlr.valid.rmse <- sqrt(mean((df_valid$log_price - mlr.valid.pred)^2))

cat("\n--- Multiple Linear Regression (Validation) ---\n")
cat("Validation R-squared:", round(mlr.valid.r2, 4), "\n")
cat("Validation RMSE:", round(mlr.valid.rmse, 4), "\n")

# Test
mlr.test.pred <- predict(mlr.fit, newdata = df_test)
mlr.test.r2 <- cor(df_test$log_price, mlr.test.pred)^2
mlr.test.rmse <- sqrt(mean((df_test$log_price - mlr.test.pred)^2))

cat("\n--- Multiple Linear Regression (Test) ---\n")
cat("Test R-squared:", round(mlr.test.r2, 4), "\n")
cat("Test RMSE:", round(mlr.test.rmse, 4), "\n")


# ============================================================
# PART C: LOGISTIC REGRESSION
# Predict price_high (high price vs low price)
# ============================================================

logit.fit <- glm(
  price_high ~ neighbourhood_group + room_type + latitude + longitude +
    minimum_nights + number_of_reviews + reviews_per_month +
    calculated_host_listings_count + availability_365 + number_of_reviews_ltm,
  data = df_train,
  family = binomial
)

summary(logit.fit)

# Validation probabilities
logit.valid.prob <- predict(logit.fit, newdata = df_valid, type = "response")
logit.valid.class <- ifelse(logit.valid.prob >= 0.5, 1, 0)

# Confusion matrix
cat("\n--- Logistic Regression (Validation Confusion Matrix) ---\n")
confusionMatrix(
  as.factor(logit.valid.class),
  df_valid$price_high,
  positive = "1"
)

# Validation AUC
logit.valid.roc <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(df_valid$price_high)), logit.valid.prob)
logit.valid.auc <- auc(logit.valid.roc)

cat("\n--- Logistic Regression (Validation) ---\n")
cat("Validation AUC:", round(logit.valid.auc, 4), "\n")

plot(logit.valid.roc, col = "blue",
     main = paste("Validation ROC - Logistic Regression (AUC =", round(logit.valid.auc, 4), ")"))

# Test probabilities
logit.test.prob <- predict(logit.fit, newdata = df_test, type = "response")
logit.test.class <- ifelse(logit.test.prob >= 0.5, 1, 0)

cat("\n--- Logistic Regression (Test Confusion Matrix) ---\n")
confusionMatrix(
  as.factor(logit.test.class),
  df_test$price_high,
  positive = "1"
)

logit.test.roc <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(df_test$price_high)), logit.test.prob)
logit.test.auc <- auc(logit.test.roc)

cat("\n--- Logistic Regression (Test) ---\n")
cat("Test AUC:", round(logit.test.auc, 4), "\n")


# ============================================================
# PART D: BINARY TREE CLASSIFICATION
# Predict price_high using classification tree
# ============================================================

tree.fit <- rpart(
  price_high ~ neighbourhood_group + room_type +
    minimum_nights + number_of_reviews + reviews_per_month +
    calculated_host_listings_count + availability_365 + number_of_reviews_ltm,
  data = df_train,
  method = "class",
  control = rpart.control(maxdepth = 5, minsplit = 100, cp = 0.001)
)

printcp(tree.fit)
plotcp(tree.fit)

# Visualise tree
rpart.plot(tree.fit, type = 2, extra = 104, fallen.leaves = TRUE)

# Validation probabilities
tree.valid.prob <- predict(tree.fit, newdata = df_valid, type = "prob")[, 2]
tree.valid.class <- ifelse(tree.valid.prob >= 0.5, 1, 0)

cat("\n--- Binary Tree (Validation Confusion Matrix) ---\n")
confusionMatrix(
  as.factor(tree.valid.class),
  df_valid$price_high,
  positive = "1"
)

tree.valid.roc <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(df_valid$price_high)), tree.valid.prob)
tree.valid.auc <- auc(tree.valid.roc)

cat("\n--- Binary Tree (Validation) ---\n")
cat("Validation AUC:", round(tree.valid.auc, 4), "\n")

plot(tree.valid.roc, col = "darkgreen",
     main = paste("Validation ROC - Binary Tree (AUC =", round(tree.valid.auc, 4), ")"))

# Test probabilities
tree.test.prob <- predict(tree.fit, newdata = df_test, type = "prob")[, 2]
tree.test.class <- ifelse(tree.test.prob >= 0.5, 1, 0)

cat("\n--- Binary Tree (Test Confusion Matrix) ---\n")
confusionMatrix(
  as.factor(tree.test.class),
  df_test$price_high,
  positive = "1"
)

tree.test.roc <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(df_test$price_high)), tree.test.prob)
tree.test.auc <- auc(tree.test.roc)

cat("\n--- Binary Tree (Test) ---\n")
cat("Test AUC:", round(tree.test.auc, 4), "\n")


# ============================================================
# PART E: Final summary table
# ============================================================

results <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Simple Linear Regression",
            "Multiple Linear Regression",
            "Logistic Regression",
            "Binary Tree"),
  Validation_Metric = c(round(slr.valid.r2, 4),
                        round(mlr.valid.r2, 4),
                        round(as.numeric(logit.valid.auc), 4),
                        round(as.numeric(tree.valid.auc), 4)),
  Test_Metric = c(round(slr.test.r2, 4),
                  round(mlr.test.r2, 4),
                  round(as.numeric(logit.test.auc), 4),
                  round(as.numeric(tree.test.auc), 4))
)

print(results)



```
